text.title: GitLab CI/CD Pipeline Design Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the gitlab-cicd methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Structure pipelines into explicit stages (build → test → scan → deploy) with needs: between jobs to keep the critical path short.
rule.rationale: Stage-only ordering serialises independent work; needs:-based DAG cuts pipeline time without losing ordering.

rule.statement: Use GitLab JWT (id_tokens) with cloud OIDC trust to mint short-lived credentials instead of CI/CD variables of type Variable.
rule.rationale: Variable-typed secrets are exfiltratable from any job logs; OIDC tokens expire in minutes.

rule.statement: Mark every long-lived credential as Protected + Masked and only expose them on protected branches and protected tags.
rule.rationale: Unprotected variables leak to merge-request pipelines from forks and feature branches.

rule.statement: Require 1+ approval on protected branches with eligible approvers restricted to a code-owners group, and block self-approval.
rule.rationale: Self-approval and arbitrary-approver patterns defeat the four-eyes rule that approvals exist to enforce.

rule.statement: Use child / parent pipelines and rules: changes to scope monorepo builds to changed paths instead of always running the whole graph.
rule.rationale: Path-scoped child pipelines turn a 30-minute monorepo build into a 5-minute targeted build on most merge requests.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
